description: The Defence Infrastructure Organisation (DIO) is the estate expert for defence, supporting the armed forces to enable military capability by planning, building, maintaining, and servicing infrastructure. DIO is part of the Ministry of Defence .

details.body:
The Defence Infrastructure Organisation (DIO) is the estate expert for defence, supporting the armed forces to enable military capability by planning, building, maintaining, and servicing infrastructure.

DIO is part of the Ministry of Defence.

image.alt_text: One of the newly installed targets, delivered by Topbond PLC, which will form an integral part of the Target 10 development (Landmacr 2026)

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Two new repurposed marine vessel targets have been successfully delivered and installed at Holbeach Air Weapons Range (AWR).
ordered_featured_documents.title: New targets installed at Holbeach to enhance Defence training

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DIO and partners have restored the Bulford Kiwi, a chalk monument on Salisbury Plain carved by New Zealand soldiers after World War 1.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Well-loved Wiltshire Kiwi gets makeover

image.alt_text: A group of people in high vis stand in a line on an open grassy area. Behind them is a digger. Three of them hold shovels and there is a sign propped against them reading "Prometheus Wave 2 - Bassingbourn Barracks".

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Defence Infrastructure Organisation, working with Custom Solar on behalf of the British Army, held a ground-breaking ceremony for a new solar array.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Ground-breaking for new solar array at Bassingbourn Barracks

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The MOD is reminding the public to follow safe access rules when visiting military training areas, to keep everyone safe and avoid disrupting training.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Respect the Range: How following access rules strengthens UK Defence

image.alt_text: A large group of soldiers stand on one side of a big gym, behind cardio equipment. They are getting ready to train.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A state-of-the-art gym has officially opened at Kendrew Barracks, Cottesmore, providing improved fitness facilities for around 1,800 soldiers and their families.
ordered_featured_documents.title: New gym opens to boost soldier wellbeing at Kendrew Barracks

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DIO is delivering a £28 million project to upgrade the airfield at RAF Coningsby, in Lincolnshire
ordered_featured_documents.title: Upgrade works under way on airfield at RAF Coningsby

details.body:
The Chief Executive (CE) Defence Infrastructure Organisation (DIO) is responsible for leading the DIO and its Executive Team. CE is responsible for developing and delivering against infrastructure objectives and deliverables set out in the Defence Plan, Defence Infrastructure Direction and Command Infrastructure Delivery Plans.

The CE’s responsibilities include:

  • ensuring a safe and legally compliant estate
  • setting the strategic plan and objectives for the organisation
  • accounting to the Service Chiefs for agreement and subsequent delivery for their programme of work and provision of service
  • implementing the 10-year strategy
  • ensuring an aligned long-term workforce strategy
Carol Bernard joined the Government Property Agency in June 2022 as Non-Executive Director where she brings a wealth of change management and stakeholder engagement experience to the GPA Board.

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a member of the Department for Education board, audit and risk committee, implementation committee and the department’s leadership team. The COO is responsible for:

  • effective management of the department’s resources
  • delivery and improvement of the main corporate functions including analysis, international education, data, technology, human resources, finance and commercial
  • governance and relationship with DfE’s non-departmental public bodies

Non-departmental responsibilities include non-executive member of Palace of Westminster, Northern Estate Programme.

role_appointments.title: Mike Green - Chief Operating Officer, Operations Group

details.body: The Director of the Capital Directorate is responsible for managing the delivery of building and maintenance programmes for schools and sixth-form colleges. This includes project managing new builds for schools in greatest need.
